notes: Spec variant 1: after a clean exit of session S, gemini is relaunched with the same session UUID (--resume <UUID>) and the same cwd. Gemini appends to the ORIGINAL chats/session-*.jsonl, so the same daemon session_id (file basename) reappears (not a new row); state is ready initially; the first new turn drives ready->working->ready. The arc is asserted per PID lifetime. daemon=full is PROVISIONAL until record confirms resume reuses the same transcript filename. BACKFLOW (recorded 2026-06-12): VERIFIED resume reuses the SAME chats/session-*.jsonl basename — exactly ONE real session_id appears across both PID lifetimes (daemon=full confirmed, no longer provisional). Observed model is gemini-3.5-flash (the API-key/AI-Studio tier default), not the OAuth-tier gemini-3-flash-preview the assessment pinned — corrected here. v2 phases pin same_session_as:v1_session_birth so the resumed turn must fire on the SAME session row, not a new one.
